Documentation comment not found for arrow function `MetaRow`


It is recommended to have documentation comments above, or right inside a function/class declaration. This helps developers, users and even the author understand the purpose of a code snippet or API function in the future. **NOTE**: If you want to stop this issue from getting raised on certain constructs (arrow functions, class expressions, methods etc.), consider using the [skip_doc_coverage](https://docs.deepsource.com/docs/analyzers-javascript#meta) option under the `analyzers.meta` property in your `.deepsource.toml` file. For example, the following configuration will silence this issue for class expressions and method definitions: ```TOML [analyzers.meta] skip_doc_coverage = ["class-expression", "method-definition"] ```

`MonitorControls` has a cyclomatic complexity of 27 with "very-high" risk


A function with high cyclomatic complexity can be hard to understand and maintain. Cyclomatic complexity is a software metric that measures the number of independent paths through a function. A higher cyclomatic complexity indicates that the function has more decision points and is more complex.

JSX tree is too deeply nested. Found 5 levels of nesting


Nesting JSX elements too deeply can confuse developers reading the code. To make maintenance and refactoring easier, DeepSource recommends limiting the maximum JSX tree depth to 4.

Unexpected string concatenation


In ES2015 (ES6), we can use template literals instead of string concatenation.

Unexpected string concatenation


In ES2015 (ES6), we can use template literals instead of string concatenation.

'GeoJsonFeature' is defined but never used


Unused variables are generally considered a code smell and should be avoided.

'/code/src/components/Map/OpenLayersForecastMap.tsx' imported multiple times


It is always recommended to group all imports of a single path or source in a single import declaration. These usually happen in a codebase when multiple developers are working on the same codebase. It helps improving code refactoring and code readability as well.

'/code/src/monitor/types.ts' imported multiple times

Arrow function expected no return value


Any code paths that do not have explicit returns will return `undefined`. It is recommended to replace any implicit dead-ends that return `undefined` with a `return null` statement.

Remove redundant `undefined` from function call


When an argument is omitted from a function call, it will default to `undefined`. It is therefore redundant to explicitly pass an `undefined` literal as the last argument.

[//]: # (cs-code-health)
Γ¥î New issue: [**Complex Method**](https://codescene.io/projects/75194/delta?repo-id=1389917&review-id=323&biomarker=Complex+Method&filename=src%2Fauth%2FAuthProvider.tsx&method=useHostedAuthState.updateSyncedSettings)
useHostedAuthState.updateSyncedSettings has a cyclomatic complexity of 10, threshold = 10

<sub>[Suppress](https://codescene.io/projects/75194/delta?repo-id=1389917&review-id=323&biomarker=Complex+Method&filename=src%2Fauth%2FAuthProvider.tsx&method=useHostedAuthState.updateSyncedSettings&suppress=true)</sub>

**`statement` and `other` alerts silently inherit the advisories toggle** `filterNwsAlertCollection` handles `watch`, `warning`, and `advisory` with their own guards, then falls through with `return options.showAdvisories` for both `statement` and `other`. Since `alertsShowAdvisories` defaults to `false`, every Special Weather Statement, Hazardous Weather Outlook, and uncategorized alert is hidden by default ΓÇö and users have no toggle to reveal them. The `alertColors` map already treats `statement` and `other` as distinct visual categories, so this control mismatch is unintended. All `statement`/`other` features should either always be shown or get their own filter flag. ```suggestion if (category === 'advisory') { return options.showAdvisories; } // 'statement' and 'other' have no dedicated toggle; always show them. return true; ```
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/monitor/nwsAlerts.ts Line: 55-63 Comment: **`statement` and `other` alerts silently inherit the advisories toggle** `filterNwsAlertCollection` handles `watch`, `warning`, and `advisory` with their own guards, then falls through with `return options.showAdvisories` for both `statement` and `other`. Since `alertsShowAdvisories` defaults to `false`, every Special Weather Statement, Hazardous Weather Outlook, and uncategorized alert is hidden by default ΓÇö and users have no toggle to reveal them. The `alertColors` map already treats `statement` and `other` as distinct visual categories, so this control mismatch is unintended. All `statement`/`other` features should either always be shown or get their own filter flag. ```suggestion if (category === 'advisory') { return options.showAdvisories; } // 'statement' and 'other' have no dedicated toggle; always show them. return true; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

**`useLocalMonitorSettings` overwrites localStorage with defaults on every mount** Effects run in definition order. Effect 1 sets `hydratedRef.current = true` and dispatches the hydrated settings, but `dispatch` only schedules a re-render ΓÇö it does not update the `settings` prop before Effect 2 runs. Effect 2 therefore executes on first mount with `settings === DEFAULT_MONITOR_SETTINGS` while `hydratedRef.current` is already `true`, so it calls `writeStoredMonitorSettings(DEFAULT_MONITOR_SETTINGS)` and overwrites the user's stored settings. A second render restores the correct value, but if the tab is closed during that gap (which occurs on every `/monitor` navigation), the user loses their saved radar site, zoom, opacity preferences, etc.
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/pages/MonitorPage.tsx Line: 52-73 Comment: **`useLocalMonitorSettings` overwrites localStorage with defaults on every mount** Effects run in definition order. Effect 1 sets `hydratedRef.current = true` and dispatches the hydrated settings, but `dispatch` only schedules a re-render ΓÇö it does not update the `settings` prop before Effect 2 runs. Effect 2 therefore executes on first mount with `settings === DEFAULT_MONITOR_SETTINGS` while `hydratedRef.current` is already `true`, so it calls `writeStoredMonitorSettings(DEFAULT_MONITOR_SETTINGS)` and overwrites the user's stored settings. A second render restores the correct value, but if the tab is closed during that gap (which occurs on every `/monitor` navigation), the user loses their saved radar site, zoom, opacity preferences, etc. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

**Filter-option changes trigger unnecessary NWS API refetches and produce stale animation frames** `filterOptions` is in the dependency array of the initial fetch `useEffect` (line 106). Every toggle of `showWatches`, `showWarnings`, or `showAdvisories` triggers a fresh HTTP request to `api.weather.gov/alerts/active`, even though filtering is applied client-side after the response. More importantly, old frames already in the `frames` array were filtered with the previous `filterOptions`, so once the new frame is appended, animation loops show differently filtered snapshots across frames. The raw collection should be stored and filtering applied in `activeCollection` via `useMemo`.
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/monitor/useMonitorNwsAlerts.ts Line: 64-106 Comment: **Filter-option changes trigger unnecessary NWS API refetches and produce stale animation frames** `filterOptions` is in the dependency array of the initial fetch `useEffect` (line 106). Every toggle of `showWatches`, `showWarnings`, or `showAdvisories` triggers a fresh HTTP request to `api.weather.gov/alerts/active`, even though filtering is applied client-side after the response. More importantly, old frames already in the `frames` array were filtered with the previous `filterOptions`, so once the new frame is appended, animation loops show differently filtered snapshots across frames. The raw collection should be stored and filtering applied in `activeCollection` via `useMemo`. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

**`setFrameIndex` called inside a `setFrames` updater ΓÇö side effect in a state updater** Calling one `setState` inside another state updater function is an anti-pattern. React Strict Mode (development build) intentionally invokes updaters twice to surface these side effects, which would cause `setFrameIndex` to be called twice per state update, potentially leaving `frameIndex` one position ahead of the correct value. The `setFrameIndex` call should happen immediately after `setFrames` resolves, not inside the updater.
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/monitor/useMonitorNwsAlerts.ts Line: 79-98 Comment: **`setFrameIndex` called inside a `setFrames` updater ΓÇö side effect in a state updater** Calling one `setState` inside another state updater function is an anti-pattern. React Strict Mode (development build) intentionally invokes updaters twice to surface these side effects, which would cause `setFrameIndex` to be called twice per state update, potentially leaving `frameIndex` one position ahead of the correct value. The `setFrameIndex` call should happen immediately after `setFrames` resolves, not inside the updater. How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Missing `response.ok` check means a non-2xx JSON response from the server is cached as valid GeoJSON, permanently breaking state-outline rendering for the session. ```suggestion if (!cachedUsStatesGeoJSON) { const response = await fetch(US_STATES_GEOJSON_URL); if (!response.ok) { throw new Error(`Failed to fetch US states GeoJSON: ${response.statusText}`); } cachedUsStatesGeoJSON = await response.json() as object; } ```
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/components/Monitor/MonitorMap.tsx Line: 145-148 Comment: Missing `response.ok` check means a non-2xx JSON response from the server is cached as valid GeoJSON, permanently breaking state-outline rendering for the session. ```suggestion if (!cachedUsStatesGeoJSON) { const response = await fetch(US_STATES_GEOJSON_URL); if (!response.ok) { throw new Error(`Failed to fetch US states GeoJSON: ${response.statusText}`); } cachedUsStatesGeoJSON = await response.json() as object; } ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````
